title: Cladopus hookeriana
taxonomy: Cladopus hookeriana is classified within the family Peridiscaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the "bastard bittersweet" or "water bittersweet" family. This plant belongs to the genus Cladopus, which is a small group of aquatic plants. Cladopus hookeriana is further classified as a species within the genus, with its classification being Cladopus hookeriana (Hook.) Pax. The family Peridiscaceae is a part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as the angiosperms. The classification of Cladopus hookeriana reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as an aquatic plant.

intro: Cladopus hookeriana, also known as Hooker's waterwort, is a rare and endemic plant species found in Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Phytotrichaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cladopus hookeriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cladopus hookeriana is often prepared into infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to treat various ailments, with the most common preparations being Hooker's waterwort tea and Hooker's waterwort extract capsules.
